Interface StageCreator

public interface StageCreator
A factory interface for controlled creation and customization of Stage instances.

Direct instantiation of Stage via constructor is restricted because:

  • Stages are tightly bound to a RenderingContext and must be managed by the graphics system
  • Uncontrolled creation could lead to resource leaks or inconsistent state

Implementations of this interface provide:

  1. The Assets bundle required for loading images, sounds, and other media during stage setup
  2. Custom configuration logic applied after the stage is constructed

This pattern ensures that stages are:

  • Created with proper dependencies
  • Configured in a consistent, testable way
  • Integrated safely into the rendering lifecycle

Example usage

public class MyGameStage implements StageCreator {

    private static final Assets STAGE_ASSETS = ...;
    private static final Assets PLAYER_ASSETS = ...;
    // ...

    @Override
    public Assets getStageAssets() {
        return STAGE_ASSETS;
    }

    @Override
    public void customize(Stage stage) {
        stage.setBackdrop("sky_blue");

        Sprite player = new Sprite(PLAYER_ASSETS);
        player.setCostume("idle");
        stage.registerSprite("player", player);

        // ...
    }
}
See Also:
  • RenderingContext.getStage()

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    customize(Stage stage)
    Customizes the given stage after construction.
    Assets
    getStageAssets()
    Returns the assets bundle used by this stage (backdrops and sounds).

  • Method Details

    • getStageAssets

      Assets getStageAssets()
      Returns the assets bundle used by this stage (backdrops and sounds).
      Returns:
      the non-null assets bundle for this stage

    • customize

      void customize(Stage stage)
      Customizes the given stage after construction.

      This method is called immediately after the stage is instantiated, allowing implementors to:

      • Add sprites and UI elements
      • Set backdrop
      • Register event listeners
      • Configure physics or camera settings

      Note: The stage is already fully constructed and safe to use, but has not yet been rendered. This is the ideal time to set up initial state.

      Parameters:
      stage - the stage to customize, never null
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if stage is null
